title: anemone obtusiloba subsp. potentilloides
intro: Anemone obtusiloba subsp. potentilloides, also known as the potentilla-leaved anemone, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Anemone obtusiloba subsp. potentilloides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the roots or leaves in water or ethanol to create a medicinal tea or tincture.
